title: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the Israel and Hezbollah, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli Government and Hezbollah. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A U.S.-brokered 10-day cessation of hostilities between Israel and Lebanon took effect April 16, 2026, amid the ongoing 2026 Lebanon war that escalated March 2 with Hezbollah rocket attacks and Israeli airstrikes in response to the Israel-Iran conflict. Direct talks in Washington between Lebanese and Israeli officials marked a rare diplomatic breakthrough, though Hezbollah— not formally party to the agreement—insisted on curbs to Israeli operations, amid mutual accusations of minor violations. The truce has largely held through day four, but Israeli PM Netanyahu emphasized continued military action against Hezbollah threats. Key upcoming events include negotiations for a permanent security deal by April 26 and parallel U.S.-Iran diplomacy that could enable de-escalation or renewed escalation.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a narrow edge in trader consensus at 16% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, fueled by their unbeaten UEFA qualifying run—topping their group—and lingering momentum from the Euro 2024 title, driven by Lamine Yamal's emergence and midfield depth. France sits tight at 15.8%, surging to the top of FIFA rankings after dominant March qualifier victories featuring Kylian Mbappé's clinical finishing. The closely contested odds underscore a stacked 48-team draw, where England (10.9%), Argentina (8.8%), and Brazil (8.5%) also topped confederation tables with proven knockout resilience; the format's round-of-32 progression amplifies variance from group stage surprises, rest advantages, and squad rotations in the June tournament across North America.

title: Rockets vs. Lakers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 18 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Rockets win, the market will resolve to "Rockets".
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lakers hold a 1-0 lead in their Western Conference first-round NBA playoffs series after a 107-98 Game 1 victory over the Rockets on April 18 at Crypto.com Arena, fueled by Luke Kennard's career playoff-high 27 points off the bench and LeBron James' 19 points with 13 assists despite missing Luka Doncic (Grade 2 hamstring strain, out indefinitely) and Austin Reaves (Grade 2 oblique, 4-6 weeks). Houston lacked Kevin Durant (knee contusion), alongside season-ending absences of Fred VanVleet (ACL tear) and Steven Adams (ankle surgery), exposing depth issues in a tight 4-vs-5 matchup between 53-29 LA and 52-30 HOU. Game 2 looms April 21 with Lakers' home-court edge intact, though Durant's status and Houston's rebounding could shift momentum.

In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 18 at 6:00 PM ET:

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold a 1-0 series lead over the Atlanta Hawks in their Eastern Conference first-round NBA playoffs matchup after a commanding 113-102 Game 1 victory at Madison Square Garden on April 18, powered by Jalen Brunson's 28 points and Karl-Anthony Towns' 25-point, dominant second-half performance. Game 2 remains at Knicks' home tomorrow, amplifying New York's home-court edge and defensive intensity that stifled Atlanta's late surge. Hawks backup center Jock Landale remains out with a right ankle sprain since early April, thinning their frontcourt rotation, while forward Mouhamed Gueye is questionable with a shoulder issue; Knicks report no major absences post-Game 1. Atlanta's end-of-regular-season streak earned the No. 6 seed, but New York's superior 53-29 record and recent head-to-head win fuel trader consensus on the higher seed's path.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 47.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions after clinching the league's best 64-18 record and home-court advantage throughout the playoffs, entering the first round fully healthy with reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander fresh off rest in the finale. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 14.5% following their surge to the No. 2 Western Conference seed behind Victor Wembanyama's dominant rookie-to-sophomore leap, setting up a favorable matchup versus the No. 7 Trail Blazers. Boston Celtics hold 12% as Eastern Conference contenders with the No. 2 seed, facing Philadelphia today amid the playoffs' April 18 tipoff, while Denver Nuggets linger at 9.5% buoyed by Nikola Jokic's triple-double averages despite a tougher path. Recent play-in resolutions and minimal injury concerns have solidified these top probabilities.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Gavin Newsom as the clear frontrunner at 27.4% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, reflecting his term-limited California governorship ending in early 2027, high-profile national attacks on the Trump administration, and recent March 2026 polls showing him trouncing Kamala Harris in a home-state primary matchup while tying for second among New Hampshire primary voters. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.3% on progressive grassroots appeal and youth, as Democrats reassess post-2024 loss, while Jon Ossoff's recent surge to 6.6% highlights Georgia's battleground value. Harris lags at 6.8% amid lingering 2024 defeat baggage. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm performances in swing states, early fundraising hauls, and endorsements from party elders, with the wide-open field lacking an incumbent.

title: Everton FC vs. Liverpool FC
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 19, 2026 between Everton FC and Liverpool FC.

markets.question: Will Everton FC win on 2026-04-19?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 19, 2026
If Everton FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Liverpool's commanding position atop the Premier League table, bolstered by a 2-0 victory over Fulham last weekend despite six senior absences including Hugo Ekitike's season-ending Achilles rupture and Joe Gomez's muscle doubt, underpins trader consensus implying virtual certainty of a win in this Merseyside derby at Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium. The Reds' superior recent form, unbeaten run in recent head-to-heads, and firepower from fit stars like Mohamed Salah outweigh Everton's home advantage and solid draw at Brentford, hampered by Jack Grealish's broken foot and Vitaliy Mykolenko's adductor issue. Realistic challenges include an early red card, injury to Virgil van Dijk or Salah, or Everton's counter-attacks exploiting Liverpool's depleted defense yielding a shock draw or upset.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran negotiations mediated by Pakistan advanced with a Phase 1 ceasefire and Iran's conditional reopening of the Strait of Hormuz on April 17, easing immediate escalation risks after U.S. port blockades and military strikes. However, core sticking points persist: Iran proposes a five-year nuclear suspension and demands full sanctions relief plus war reparations, while the U.S. seeks a 20-year halt and rejects Hormuz transit fees. No date is set for the next round amid President Trump's threats of renewed action if no deal materializes soon. Traders monitor for diplomatic breakthroughs, as Iran's economic isolation from sanctions and blockades heightens pressure, though historical mistrust and proxy conflicts in the region sustain uncertainty ahead of potential talks this week.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' sweep of the first three 2026 Grands Prix—Australia (Russell victory), China, and Japan (Antonelli win)—has propelled teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of the Drivers' Championship standings and trader consensus, with Antonelli leading by nine points at 72-63. Russell's implied 41.5% probability edges Antonelli's 30.6% among bettors, reflecting his superior racecraft, consistency, and experience against the 18-year-old rookie's breakout form as the youngest-ever points leader. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (6.1%) trails in third, while Red Bull's Max Verstappen (2.5%) and McLaren's duo languish amid new-era regulation struggles, emphasizing Mercedes' dominant power unit and chassis advantage ahead of the Miami Grand Prix.

title: Counter-Strike: FURIA vs Vitality (BO3) - IEM Rio Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 2 match between FURIA and Vitality in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 18 at 2:15PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win the match against Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Vitality" if Vitality win the match against FURIA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 2 match between FURIA and Vitality in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 18 at 2:15PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if FURIA and Vitality play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map Handicap: VIT (-1.5) vs FURIA (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 2 match between FURIA and Vitality in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 18 at 2:15PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Vitality" if Vitality wins 2 or more maps than FURIA in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FURIA". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: FURIA (-1.5) vs Vitality (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 2 match between FURIA and Vitality in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 18 at 2:15PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA wins 2 or more maps than Vitality in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Vitality". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vitality, the world #1-ranked team, entered the IEM Rio 2026 playoffs as trader consensus favorites after dominating recent S-tier events like BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 and strong showings at PGL Cluj-Napoca, with ZywOo leading via elite AWP performances and a 91% three-month win rate. FURIA, world #3, leveraged home-crowd energy in Rio—fueled by massive viewership peaks—and a three-match streak including a 2-1 upset over Natus Vincere in groups, though prior losses to Falcons and Aurora tempered expectations. Head-to-head favors Vitality 11-7 in series; key factors included map veto (Vitality's Overpass pick, FURIA's Ancient), roster stability, and crowd noise potentially disrupting CT sides in this high-stakes BO3 semifinal bracket matchup.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid remains sidelined recovering from appendectomy surgery performed April 9, officially ruled out for Game 1 of their first-round NBA playoffs matchup at the Boston Celtics, marking a critical blow to Philly's frontcourt after their play-in win over Orlando. The Celtics enter with a clean injury report, their full rotation—including Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown—available following late-season ailments, bolstering trader consensus at 88% implied probability for a Boston victory per Polymarket pricing. Home-court advantage at TD Garden, superior regular-season form, and Philly's adaptation challenges without their MVP-caliber big man underpin the lopsided sentiment, though the 7-seed Sixers' backcourt led by Tyrese Maxey poses upset potential in a best-of-seven series.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aston Villa's dominant trader consensus stems from their fourth-place standing in the Premier League table, fueling a critical Champions League qualification push, combined with a strong home record at Villa Park where they boast superior head-to-head history against Sunderland. Recent midweek European dominance and returns like Emiliano Martinez bolster Unai Emery's squad depth, despite absences including Boubacar Kamara and Ross Barkley, while mid-table Sunderland (around 10th-11th) grapples with multiple injuries such as Simon Moore and fitness concerns for key defenders like Dan Ballard. This quality gap, recent form momentum, and rest advantages post-Europe have driven implied probabilities to near-certainty for a Villa win, though Sunderland's counterattacking threat or Villa red cards could spark an upset in extra time or penalties if tied late.

title: LoL: HANJIN BRION vs Nongshim Red Force (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 19 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "HANJIN BRION" if HANJIN BRION win the match against Nongshim Red Force.

This market will resolve to "Nongshim Red Force" if Nongshim Red Force win the match against HANJIN BRION.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 19 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 1, or if Game 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 2. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: LoL: HANJIN BRION vs Nongshim Red Force (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 19 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "HANJIN BRION" if HANJIN BRION win the match against Nongshim Red Force.

This market will resolve to "Nongshim Red Force" if Nongshim Red Force win the match against HANJIN BRION.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game Handicap: BRO (-1.5) vs Nongshim Red Force (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between HANJIN BRION and Nongshim Red Force in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 19 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "HANJIN BRION" if HANJIN BRION wins 2 or more games than Nongshim Red Force in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Nongshim Red Force". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nongshim Red Force enters this LCK 2026 Rounds 1-2 BO3 with momentum at 3-2 series record and 60% game win rate, ranking third in standings after a mixed week including a 0-2 loss to Kiwoom DRX but prior wins over Dplus KIA. HANJIN BRION languishes at 1-4 (20% series win rate, 3-8 games), extending a three-series skid following a dismal 0-5 group stage at LCK Cup 2026 and recent 0-2 defeat to Gen.G. NS holds a dominant 15-6 head-to-head edge, including last year's 3-2 victory, bolstering trader sentiment amid BRION's execution struggles in drafts and objectives. Upset potential lies in BRION's top/jungle synergy exploiting any NS overextensions in the current aggressive meta.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President J.D. Vance holds a slim edge over Secretary of State Marco Rubio in recent polls like Rasmussen's April 17 survey and the late-March CPAC straw poll, where Vance garnered 43-53% support amid his heir-apparent status in the Trump administration, but prediction market traders diverge sharply, pricing HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the Republican presidential nominee frontrunner at 49% implied probability, with Vance at 39% and Rubio at 22%. This tight race reflects an open 2028 primary due to President Trump's term limits, bolstered by RFK Jr.'s Make America Healthy Again movement appealing to health policy voters, Rubio's foreign policy gains from Venezuela operations and Iran tensions, and Vance's ambiguous positioning despite Trump's public distancing. Trader sentiment stays fluid ahead of 2026 midterms, potential Trump endorsements, or administration scandals that could tip the balance.

title: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Rajasthan Royals
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals scheduled for April 19 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Rajasthan Royals - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals scheduled for 2026-04-19 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Kolkata Knight Riders vs Rajasthan Royals - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Kolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals scheduled for 2026-04-19 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rajasthan Royals hold the edge as trader consensus favorite entering IPL 2026 Match 28 at Eden Gardens, buoyed by 4 wins in 5 outings despite a dramatic top-order collapse chasing 217 against SRH five days ago that exposed middle-order frailties. Captain Riyan Parag won the toss and elected to bat first on a spinner-friendly pitch where recent afternoon games favored first innings, under clear skies with temperatures at 36°C. Winless-in-five Kolkata Knight Riders, languishing near the bottom with just 1 victory, battle bowling woes including Matheesha Pathirana's absence and prior injuries to Harshit Rana and Varun Chakaravarthy, though Sunil Narine returns; KKR boast a 7-5 head-to-head home record versus RR, with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's strike rate of 263 fueling Royals' momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's mounting injury crisis, highlighted by Bukayo Saka's confirmed Achilles absence and doubts over Martin Ødegaard, Jurrien Timber, and Riccardo Calafiori following their Champions League exertions, has elevated Manchester City's trader consensus to a 52.5% implied win probability for this pivotal Premier League Etihad showdown. The league leaders hold a six-point edge atop the table after 32 games (+38 goal difference), but a recent 1-2 upset loss to Bournemouth exposed vulnerabilities, while second-placed City (64 points from 31 games) remain unbeaten in nine league outings, including a 3-0 Chelsea romp, and boast 11 straight home unbeaten runs against Arsenal. City's own defensive woes—Rúben Dias and Joško Gvardiol out—temper favoritism, keeping Arsenal viable at 22.5% and draw likely at 26.5% in this tight title race decider.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA commands a commanding market cap lead at $4.9 trillion, nearly 20% ahead of Alphabet's $4.1 trillion and further from Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Tesla, and Saudi Aramco, driving trader consensus to a 99.2% implied probability it remains the world's largest company by April 30. This positioning stems from a 10-day winning streak adding 18% to shares last week, propelled by CEO Jensen Huang's disclosure of $1 trillion in GPU orders for AI data centers amid surging demand for Blackwell accelerators. While the wisdom of crowds reflects strong confidence in NVIDIA's AI chip dominance, realistic challenges include a sharp AI sector pullback, intensified competition from custom silicon by hyperscalers, or explosive earnings surprises from rivals in the final trading days.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus at 18.8% implied probability for the 2028 presidential election winner, buoyed by his position as heir apparent in recent CPAC straw polls where he garnered 53% among GOP voters, though a tough week including Turning Point USA controversies tempered gains. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16.9%, reflecting his rising national profile and post-2026 midterm positioning signals amid Democratic field fragmentation. Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 11.5% benefits from diplomatic wins like Venezuela's Maduro ouster and donor preference, keeping the race tight. With 2026 midterms looming as a key catalyst for primaries, economic performance under the Trump administration and party unity will likely create separation among this crowded early field.

title: Dota 2: Xtreme Gaming vs Aurora (BO3) - PGL Wallachia Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Round 2 match between Xtreme Gaming and Aurora in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Xtreme Gaming" if Xtreme Gaming win the match against Aurora.

This market will resolve to "Aurora" if Aurora win the match against Xtreme Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 36.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Round 2 match between Xtreme Gaming and Aurora in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 3:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 37 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Round 2 match between Xtreme Gaming and Aurora in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 3:00AM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game 1: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 1 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Xtreme Gaming and Aurora each kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

This market is about whether any player achieves a Rampage during Game 2. In Dota 2, a Rampage occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy heroes in rapid succession without dying.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Rampage during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Rampage.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether a Rampage occurred prior to stoppage. If no Rampage occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 3 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 3. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Xtreme Gaming and Aurora each kill Roshan at least once during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 3.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Xtreme Gaming and Aurora each destroy at least one enemy barracks (melee or ranged) during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy barracks.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Ultra Kill?

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the PGL Wallachia Season 8 Swiss group stage BO3, Xtreme Gaming enters Round 2 at 1-0 after dominating Natus Vincere 2-0 in their opener, showcasing Ame's carry prowess and NothingToSay's midlane control under patch 7.41b. Aurora Gaming matches that record with a win over South America Rejects, riding momentum from their 2-0 upset of Xtreme three weeks ago at ESL One Birmingham group stage amid mixed recent form for both squads. Head-to-head history tilts slightly to Xtreme overall (8-6), but Aurora's aggressive drafts featuring Nightfall and Mikoto have exposed vulnerabilities in Xtreme's macro play. Trader consensus views this as a coinflip matchup critical for high bracket advancement toward playoffs top 8, with draft flexibility and teamfight execution pivotal in neutral LAN conditions.

title: LoL: Bilibili Gaming vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 19 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Bilibili Gaming holds a strong 4-1 record in LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend double round robin, rebounding from a 0-2 upset loss to Top Esports on April 16 with a 2-1 victory over Invictus Gaming on April 18, bolstering their direct playoffs push. Anyone's Legend sits mid-table at 2-2, fresh off a 2-0 win versus Team WE but hampered by early laning woes and jungler Tarzan's CS deficits, as noted in recent analyst critiques. BLG's star-studded lineup—featuring top laner Bin, mid laner Knight, and ADC Viper—holds recent head-to-head edges, including 2-1 and 2-0 series wins over AL this split. No roster changes or substitutes reported ahead of this BO3 rematch.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons host the Orlando Magic in Game 1 of their first-round NBA playoffs matchup as the East's top seed, leveraging home-court advantage and a dominant regular season that nearly hit 60 wins. The Pistons enter with a clean injury report, including Cade Cunningham fully available post-lung injury and Jalen Duren unrestricted, fueling trader consensus on their edge in this physical clash against Paolo Banchero and Orlando's defense. Magic forward Jonathan Isaac remains questionable with a left knee sprain, while Wendell Carter Jr. is available despite a protective face mask; Orlando advanced via Play-In win over Charlotte but split the season series 2-2 amid Orlando's late push and Detroit's superior momentum from recent form and rest.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen lead trader consensus for Eurovision 2026 victory with a 36.5% implied probability on "Liekinheitin," their violin-pop fusion that triumphed at UMK on February 28 and has dominated fan polls and bookie odds since song releases concluded in March. France's rising star Monroe trails at 11.3% with the sleek "Regarde!," benefiting from Big 4 direct grand final entry, while Denmark's emotive ballad "Før vi går hjem" by Søren Torpegaard Lund holds 10.5% after its DMGP win. Australia's Delta Goodrem adds star power at 7% via "Eclipse," and Greece's Akylas with upbeat "Ferto" sits at 6.3%. Recent semi-final running orders announced early April and pre-parties like London Eurovision Party have solidified frontrunners, though jury votes and live rehearsals ahead of Vienna semis on May 12/14 could spark shifts in this open field.

title: LoL: Top Esports vs Invictus Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 19 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against Invictus Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Top Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Invictus Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Invictus Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Invictus Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 1, or if Game 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Invictus Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Quadra Kill?

markets.question: Game Handicap: IG (-1.5) vs Top Esports (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 19 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming wins 2 or more games than Top Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Top Esports". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
